content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the prompt-generation changes end-to-end and found three issues: an over-broad `br list` corruption heuristic that turns recoverable failures into hard errors, a planned-elsewhere summary extractor that is inconsistent with the repository’s own bead-description grammar, and a milestone progress formatter that double-counts unfinished beads.

## Amendments

1. High: `project create-from-bead` can now hard-fail on ordinary `br list` filesystem errors instead of taking the intended deterministic fallback. In `src/cli/project.rs:321-333`, generic `AppError::Io` from `load_bead_summaries` is supposed to degrade to direct relation metadata when statuses are present, but `map_br_list_error` + `br_list_exit_error_looks_corrupt` (`src/cli/project.rs:784-822`) classify any stderr mentioning `.beads/issues.jsonl` / `issues.jsonl` as `CorruptRecord`. A missing-file or permission-denied message on `.beads/issues.jsonl` will therefore bypass the fallback and abort project creation even though the prompt could still be rendered deterministically from `br show` relation statuses.
   Rationale: This is a robustness regression in the new prompt hydration path: benign `br list` failures become fatal because the corruption heuristic is too broad.
2. Medium: planned-elsewhere summaries mishandle valid bead-description section labels and can emit useless summaries like `Non-goals:` instead of real scope text. `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` only strips a small whitelist of labels in `src/cli/project.rs:1113-1127` and otherwise returns the first non-empty line in `src/cli/project.rs:1182-1195`. But the prompt system already recognizes plain `Non-goals:` / `Acceptance Criteria:` section labels as legitimate bead-description boundaries in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:520-537`. If a neighboring bead description starts with one of those supported labels, the generated `Already Planned Elsewhere` section becomes misleading instead of self-contained.
   Rationale: The new summary extractor is not aligned with the existing bead-description grammar, so valid nearby bead descriptions can collapse into label-only summaries.
3. Medium: the new milestone progress string double-counts unfinished beads and misstates milestone state. `render_bead_task_prompt` formats progress as `... in progress; ... blocked; ... remaining` using `MilestoneProgress::remaining()` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:844-854`, but `remaining()` is defined in `src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:221-226` as `total - completed - failed - skipped`, which already includes in-progress and blocked beads. That means prompts can report combinations like `1/4 completed; 1 in progress; 1 blocked; 3 remaining`, which is internally inconsistent and can mislead the agent reading only `prompt.md`.
   Rationale: This is a correctness bug in the newly added prompt content: the displayed progress numbers do not describe disjoint categories and overstate how much work is left.
